title: Chef Coin Flipping Game

content.problemStatement:
The chef is playing a game with a coin that has a 111 printed on one side and a 222 printed on the other. He wants to find out how many different ways he can achieve a sum of SSS by flipping the coin any number of times.

Help Chef determine the total number of ways to get a sum of SSS using any number of flips of the coin.


Input Format
The input consists of a single integer SSS.



Output Format
Output a single integer representing the number of ways to achieve the sum SSS.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a single integer S (1 ≤ S ≤ 20), the target sum.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer — the number of ordered sequences of coin flips that sum to S.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For S=1 only (1) → 1 way. For S=2 sequences (1,1) and (2) → 2 ways. For S=3 sequences (1,1,1), (1,2), (2,1) → 3 ways.
dp[20] follows the Fibonacci recurrence and equals 10946.

solution.approach: Each flip contributes either 1 or 2 to the total sum. The number of ordered sequences that sum to S is exactly the number of ways to write S as an ordered sum of 1s and 2s, which follows the Fibonacci recurrence: dp[0]=1 (empty sequence), dp[1]=1, and for i≥2, dp[i]=dp[i-1]+dp[i-2]. Pre‑compute dp up to the maximum possible S (20) once, then answer each test case in O(1).
solution.timeComplexity: Precomputation O(MAX) = O(20). Each query O(1). Total O(T + MAX).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(MAX) for the dp array, i.e., O(20) ≈ O(1).
